title: trifolium agrestinum subsp. lagopinum
intro: Trifolium agrestinum subsp. lagopinum, also known as Mountain Clover, is a perennial plant belonging to the legume family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help in reducing pain, swelling, and oxidative stress in the body. The health benefits of Mountain Clover include its ability to improve digestion, boost the immune system, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Mountain Clover include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
